examples.title: Cristian's Algorithm Implementation
examples.description: Implementation of client-side Cristian's algorithm for clock synchronization
examples.explanation: The algorithm sends a request to a time server, records timestamps, calculates round-trip time, estimates one-way delay as half of RTT, and adjusts the local clock accordingly. Maximum error equals half the RTT.

examples.title: Berkeley Algorithm - Master Side
examples.description: Master node implementation for Berkeley clock synchronization algorithm
examples.code:
PROCEDURE BerkeleyMasterSync(slaveAddresses)
 slaveTimes ← []
 
 // Poll all slaves for their times
 FOR EACH slave IN slaveAddresses DO
 send(TIME_REQUEST, slave)
 response ← receive()
 slaveTimes.append(response.localTime)
 END FOR
 
 // Get master's own time
 masterTime ← currentLocalTime()
 
 // Add master time to collection
 allTimes ← slaveTimes + [masterTime]
 
 // Calculate average time
 avgTime ← sum(allTimes) / length(allTimes)
 
 // Calculate adjustments for each node
 FOR EACH node IN slaveAddresses DO
 adjustment ← avgTime - node.time
 send(ADJUST, node, adjustment)
 END FOR
 
 // Adjust master if needed
 masterAdjustment ← avgTime - masterTime
 adjustClock(masterAdjustment)
 
 RETURN {avgTime, adjustmentsSent}
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE SlaveResponseHandler()
 request ← receive()
 IF request.type = TIME_REQUEST THEN
 response ← {localTime: currentLocalTime()}
 send(response, request.sender)
 ELSE IF request.type = ADJUST THEN
 adjustClock(request.adjustment)
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: The master polls all slaves, computes the average of all clock times (including its own), calculates the adjustment needed for each machine, and sends the adjustments to all nodes.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of nodes

examples.title: NTP Client Implementation
examples.description: Simplified NTP client showing the four-message exchange and offset calculation
examples.explanation: NTP uses a four-message exchange to calculate both clock offset and round-trip delay. The offset is the average of two one-way estimates. Multiple servers are used for reliability and accuracy.
examples.timeComplexity: O(k) where k is number of servers
